content.problemStatement:
A person is said to be sleep deprived if he slept **strictly less than** 777 hours in a day.

Chef was only able to sleep XXX hours yesterday. Determine if he is sleep deprived or not.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains one integer XXX — the number of hours Chef slept.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if Chef is sleep-deprived. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the number of hours Chef slept.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output "YES" if X is strictly less than 7, otherwise output "NO". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For X=4, 4<7 so Chef is sleep‑deprived → YES. For X=7 and X=10, the hours are not strictly less than 7 → NO.
7 is not strictly less than 7, so output is NO.

solution.approach: For each test case read X and compare it with the threshold 7. If X < 7 print YES, else print NO. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.
